title: AZ-900 Mock Exam 1: Cloud Foundations and Core Services
description: A 50-question unofficial mock paper aligned to the AZ-900 skills measured from July 20, 2026. Uses realistic dropdown, statement, single-choice, and multiple-answer formats with original questions and official Microsoft Learn references.

items.prompt: {{blank}} describes paying for cloud resources as they are used instead of buying datacenter capacity in advance.
items.explanation: Consumption-based pricing makes operating expense track actual use and lets a customer release resources it no longer needs. Capital expenditure pays up front for an asset, while the other choices describe availability or capacity.

items.prompt: For each task in a public-cloud virtual machine deployment, decide whether the customer is normally responsible.

statements.text: Configure access to the application
Replace failed disks in the Azure datacenter
Patch the physical host operating system

items.explanation: The customer secures its application and identities. Microsoft operates the physical datacenter, replaces host hardware, and maintains the virtualization host beneath an Azure virtual machine.

items.prompt: A company keeps regulated systems in its own datacenter and runs customer-facing services in Azure. Which TWO statements describe this design?
answers: The company remains responsible for its on-premises infrastructure
options: The company remains responsible for its on-premises infrastructure
It is exclusively a public-cloud deployment
Microsoft patches the company’s on-premises servers
items.explanation: Hybrid cloud combines private or on-premises resources with public-cloud services. The ownership boundary does not transfer maintenance of equipment that remains in the company’s datacenter to Microsoft.

items.prompt: A function runs when a queue message arrives, scales automatically, and exposes no server operating system to the developer. Which computing model is this?
items.explanation: Serverless computing lets a team run event-driven code while the provider manages host provisioning and scaling. The scenario does not describe privately owned infrastructure, a dedicated server, or a hosted desktop.

items.prompt: Who is responsible for classifying confidential customer records stored in a managed Azure database?
items.answer: The organization that owns and uses the data
options: The organization that owns and uses the data
The database engine without human policy
items.explanation: Data ownership and classification remain customer responsibilities in every cloud service model. Microsoft secures the underlying service, but it cannot decide the business sensitivity of the customer’s records.

items.prompt: {{blank}} is demonstrated when a service continues to meet its uptime target after one component fails.
items.explanation: High availability concerns keeping a service accessible despite component failures or maintenance. The alternatives describe financial treatment, information governance, or isolation rather than uptime.

references.title: Describe the benefits of using cloud services

items.prompt: Evaluate each statement about scaling and elasticity.

statements.text: Horizontal scaling can add more instances
Vertical scaling always removes instances when demand falls
Elasticity can add and remove capacity in response to demand

items.explanation: Horizontal scaling changes the number of instances, while vertical scaling changes the capacity of an existing instance. Elasticity describes responsive expansion and contraction rather than a one-way resize.

references.title: Describe the benefits of using cloud services

items.prompt: A retailer automatically adds application instances for a flash sale and removes them after traffic returns to normal. Which benefit is illustrated?
items.explanation: Elasticity matches capacity to changing demand in both directions. Governance applies rules, predictability supports forecasting, and ownership of physical capital is not the cloud behavior shown.

references.title: Describe the benefits of using cloud services

items.prompt: A team deploys the same tested template for development, test, and production. Which cloud benefit does this most directly improve?
items.explanation: Repeatable templates make environments easier to deploy and control consistently, which is a manageability benefit. Templates alone do not determine data location, guarantee availability, or authenticate users.

references.title: Describe the benefits of using cloud services

items.prompt: Developers use a {{blank}} model when they deploy code while the provider maintains the operating system and runtime.
items.explanation: PaaS provides a managed application platform, so developers focus on code and data instead of patching the host operating system and runtime. IaaS exposes more administration, while SaaS supplies a complete application.

items.prompt: For each customer requirement, decide whether infrastructure as a service (IaaS) is the best match.

statements.text: Install a legacy component that requires operating-system administrator access
Use a complete payroll application without managing its code
Deploy an API while the provider patches the runtime

items.explanation: IaaS is appropriate when the customer needs guest operating-system control. A complete business application is SaaS, and a managed application runtime is a PaaS characteristic.

items.prompt: Which TWO responsibilities normally remain with a customer that runs an application on Azure virtual machines?
answers: Configure the application and its data
options: Configure the application and its data
items.explanation: Virtual machines are IaaS: the customer manages the guest operating system, installed software, configuration, and data. Microsoft operates the physical hosts, network fabric, and hypervisor.

items.prompt: Employees use a provider-hosted email application in a browser and never manage its servers. Which service type is being consumed?
items.explanation: SaaS delivers a complete provider-managed application to users. IaaS exposes infrastructure for customer administration, while PaaS provides a development and hosting platform rather than finished email software.

items.prompt: A database team wants automated patching and backups but must retain control of schemas, queries, and stored data. Which service model is the closest fit?
items.explanation: A managed database is commonly PaaS: the provider operates the platform while the customer manages database design and data. IaaS would leave more patching to the customer, and SaaS would expose a finished application.

items.prompt: {{blank}} is the Azure scope that can organize several subscriptions so policy and access assignments inherit to them.
items.explanation: Management groups sit above subscriptions in the Azure resource hierarchy and support inherited governance. Resource groups exist inside one subscription, while zones and virtual networks are not governance containers.

items.prompt: Evaluate each statement about resource groups.

statements.text: A resource belongs to one resource group at a time
Every resource in a resource group must be in the same Azure region
A resource group can contain resources with a shared lifecycle

items.explanation: Resource groups are logical lifecycle and management containers within a subscription. They can include resources deployed in different regions, but an individual resource has one resource-group membership at a time.

items.prompt: A solution needs physically separate datacenter locations inside one Azure region, each with independent power and cooling. What should it use?
items.explanation: Availability zones are fault-isolated physical locations within a supported region. Management groups, tags, and policy organize or govern resources but do not create datacenter-level isolation.

items.prompt: A company needs an independent billing and access-management boundary for a new business unit. Which Azure scope should it create?
items.explanation: Subscriptions are Azure billing and access-control boundaries below management groups. Zones provide resiliency, locks protect changes, and storage containers organize data rather than accounts and governance.

items.prompt: Use Azure {{blank}} for an event-driven task that should run without managing servers.
items.explanation: Azure Functions provides serverless, event-driven compute and manages the underlying hosts. Virtual Desktop delivers user desktops, DNS resolves names, and Data Box transfers large offline datasets.

references.title: Describe Azure compute and networking services

items.prompt: For each requirement, decide whether Azure App Service is an appropriate solution.

statements.text: Host a web API on a managed application platform
Provide a dedicated private circuit from an office to Azure
Deliver complete Windows desktops to remote employees

items.explanation: App Service is managed hosting for web apps and APIs. ExpressRoute supplies a private circuit, while Azure Virtual Desktop supplies hosted Windows desktops and applications.

references.title: Describe Azure compute and networking services

items.prompt: An organization needs private connectivity between an on-premises network and Azure. Which TWO services can provide that connectivity?
items.explanation: VPN Gateway can establish an encrypted tunnel over the internet, while ExpressRoute provides private dedicated connectivity through a provider. DNS resolves names and Cost Management analyzes spending.

references.title: Describe Azure compute and networking services

items.prompt: A stateless web tier needs identical virtual machines that can scale as demand changes. Which Azure service is designed for this pattern?
items.explanation: Virtual Machine Scale Sets manage a group of load-balanced VM instances and support scaling. Files is storage, Purview governs data, and Service Health reports platform incidents.

references.title: Describe Azure compute and networking services

items.prompt: Two Azure virtual networks must exchange traffic privately over Microsoft’s backbone. Which feature should be configured?
items.explanation: Virtual network peering connects virtual networks so resources can communicate over private Azure routing. DNS, recommendations, and lifecycle policies do not establish network connectivity.

references.title: Describe Azure compute and networking services

items.prompt: Data that consists of images, backups, and other unstructured objects belongs in Azure {{blank}} Storage.
items.explanation: Blob Storage is Azure object storage for large amounts of unstructured data. Queues carry messages, Tables store schemaless key-value entities, and managed disks back virtual machines.

items.prompt: Evaluate each statement about Azure Storage redundancy.

statements.text: Locally redundant storage keeps copies in one datacenter
Zone-redundant storage depends on only one availability zone
Geo-redundant storage copies data to a secondary region

items.explanation: LRS protects within one datacenter, ZRS synchronously uses multiple availability zones, and GRS adds asynchronous replication to a paired secondary region. Each option addresses a different failure scope.

items.prompt: Which TWO tools are specifically useful when moving or synchronizing file data with Azure Storage?
answers: Azure File Sync for caching an Azure file share on Windows Server
options: Azure File Sync for caching an Azure file share on Windows Server
Azure Policy for copying file contents
Azure Advisor for mounting a file share
items.explanation: AzCopy performs high-performance storage data transfers, while Azure File Sync synchronizes Azure file shares with Windows Servers. Policy evaluates configuration and Advisor makes recommendations; neither transfers files.

items.prompt: A company must transfer hundreds of terabytes to Azure, but the site has insufficient network bandwidth. What should it use?
items.explanation: Azure Data Box provides Microsoft-managed appliances for large offline data transfers when a network upload is impractical. Monitoring, name resolution, and identity services do not transport the dataset.

items.prompt: Compliance records are almost never read and can tolerate several hours before retrieval. Which Blob Storage access tier best minimizes storage cost?
items.explanation: The archive tier offers the lowest storage cost for rarely accessed blobs in exchange for rehydration delay and higher retrieval cost. Hot and premium options prioritize frequent or low-latency access.

items.prompt: Requiring a password plus a verification prompt in an authenticator app is {{blank}}.
items.explanation: MFA requires evidence from more than one authentication factor, reducing reliance on a stolen password. SSO reduces repeated sign-ins, while tagging and replication are unrelated to identity verification.

references.title: Describe Azure identity, access, and security

items.prompt: Which TWO practices are central to a Zero Trust strategy?
answers: Grant only the least privilege required
options: Grant only the least privilege required
Trust requests because they originate on a corporate network
Give every administrator permanent Owner access
items.explanation: Zero Trust treats network location as insufficient proof, verifies each request with relevant signals, and limits access scope and duration. Implicit trust and broad standing privilege conflict with that approach.

references.title: Describe Azure identity, access, and security

items.prompt: Which Microsoft cloud service supplies identities and authentication for Azure resources and Microsoft 365?
items.explanation: Microsoft Entra ID is the cloud identity and access service used by Azure and Microsoft 365. The other services handle telemetry, name resolution, or storage administration.

references.title: Describe Azure identity, access, and security

items.prompt: A partner should use an existing external identity to access one selected business application. Which capability supports this?
items.explanation: External identity collaboration lets organizations invite and govern partner access without creating a separate local password for every partner. The alternatives concern compute resilience, storage, or data transfer.

references.title: Describe Azure identity, access, and security

items.prompt: A user needs read-only access to virtual machines in one resource group. What is the most appropriate assignment?
items.answer: The Reader Azure RBAC role at the resource-group scope
options: The Reader Azure RBAC role at the resource-group scope
The Owner role at the subscription scope
A delete lock on each virtual machine
A department tag on the resource group
items.explanation: Azure RBAC grants actions at a defined scope; Reader on the resource group supplies view access to its resources. Owner is excessive, and locks or tags do not grant user permissions.

references.title: Describe Azure identity, access, and security

items.prompt: To estimate the price of a proposed Azure solution before deploying it, use the Azure {{blank}}.
items.explanation: The Pricing calculator estimates expected charges for a planned combination of Azure services and usage. Service Health, Policy, and the activity log report operational or governance information rather than forecast prices.

items.prompt: Which TWO actions can directly reduce cost for predictable or idle compute workloads?
answers: Purchase a reservation for eligible steady usage
Stop and deallocate development virtual machines when unused
options: Purchase a reservation for eligible steady usage
Stop and deallocate development virtual machines when unused
Add more resource tags without changing usage
Apply a read-only lock to every virtual machine
items.explanation: Reservations can discount eligible long-running usage, and deallocation stops compute billing for unused VMs. Tags help allocation and locks protect configuration, but neither alone reduces consumed compute.

items.prompt: A finance team needs actual Azure spend grouped by department, but resources span many resource groups. What should teams apply consistently?
items.explanation: Tags attach business metadata that Cost Management can use to group and allocate charges. Zone numbers, addresses, and locks do not express department ownership in cost reports.

items.prompt: Which network activity is most likely to add a data-transfer charge to an Azure bill?
items.answer: Sending data out of Azure to the internet
options: Sending data out of Azure to the internet
Uploading data into Azure in a typical scenario
Viewing a dashboard in the Azure portal
items.explanation: Outbound data transfer is a common pricing factor, with charges varying by destination and volume. Resource names and normal portal administration do not represent metered application data egress.

items.prompt: Evaluate each statement about Azure Policy and Azure RBAC.

statements.text: Azure Policy can report resources that do not meet a rule
Azure RBAC determines whether a user may delete a resource
Azure Policy is the service that authenticates a user password

items.explanation: Policy evaluates resource configuration and compliance, whereas RBAC authorizes management actions at Azure scopes. User authentication is supplied by an identity service such as Microsoft Entra ID.

references.title: Describe Azure governance and compliance tools

items.prompt: Which TWO controls can help prevent an accidental noncompliant production change?
answers: An Azure Policy assignment that restricts resource configuration
A resource lock that blocks deletion or modification
options: An Azure Policy assignment that restricts resource configuration
A resource lock that blocks deletion or modification
A resource tag used only for reporting
A pricing estimate saved before deployment
items.explanation: Policy evaluates or restricts configuration, while locks can block destructive management operations. Reporting metadata and cost estimates do not enforce the production resource state.

references.title: Describe Azure governance and compliance tools

items.prompt: A company wants every new resource limited to an approved list of Azure regions. Which service should enforce the rule?
items.explanation: Azure Policy can deny or flag resource configurations that fall outside assigned rules, including allowed locations. DNS, recommendations, and serverless code are not the native governance control for this requirement.

references.title: Describe Azure governance and compliance tools

items.prompt: Which service provides data discovery, classification, and governance across an organization’s data estate?
items.explanation: Microsoft Purview provides data governance capabilities such as cataloging, classification, and lineage. The other options deliver desktops, network connectivity, or message storage.

references.title: Describe Azure governance and compliance tools

items.prompt: An administrator wants an authenticated browser shell with both Azure CLI and Azure PowerShell available. What should they open?
items.explanation: Cloud Shell supplies a browser-based authenticated shell with Azure command-line tools. The other services report health, transfer offline data, or monitor application behavior.

references.title: Describe tools for managing and deploying Azure resources

items.prompt: A team needs declarative deployments that can be reviewed in source control and repeated across environments. Which approach should it use?
items.explanation: Infrastructure as code captures desired resources in versioned definitions for repeatable, reviewable deployment. Tags and budgets are metadata and financial controls, while manual-only work is not a reusable definition.

references.title: Describe tools for managing and deploying Azure resources

items.prompt: Which Azure control plane processes resource-management requests from the portal, CLI, PowerShell, templates, and REST APIs?
items.explanation: Azure Resource Manager is the common management and deployment layer for Azure resources. The other services resolve names, host desktops, or move data and are managed through that control plane.

references.title: Describe tools for managing and deploying Azure resources

items.prompt: A company wants Azure governance and inventory for servers located on-premises and in another cloud. Which service should it use?
items.explanation: Azure Arc projects supported non-Azure resources into Azure management so they can use inventory, policy, and related services. Files, Advisor, and ExpressRoute do not provide that hybrid control plane.

references.title: Describe tools for managing and deploying Azure resources

items.prompt: Which language provides a concise Azure-native syntax that is compiled into an ARM template?
items.explanation: Bicep is a declarative language for Azure resource deployment and compiles to ARM template JSON. KQL queries telemetry, AzCopy transfers data, and Entra ID manages identity.

references.title: Describe tools for managing and deploying Azure resources

items.prompt: Which service provides personalized recommendations about Azure cost, reliability, security, operational excellence, and performance?
items.explanation: Azure Advisor analyzes deployed resources and reports optimization recommendations across several pillars. DNS, offline transfer, and data governance do not provide this recommendation set.

items.prompt: An Azure incident is affecting resources in the company’s subscriptions. Which service shows personalized impact and updates?
items.explanation: Service Health reports active incidents, planned maintenance, and advisories relevant to a customer’s subscriptions. The alternatives estimate cost, assess configuration, or administer storage.

items.prompt: Operations needs an email when a virtual machine’s CPU metric remains above a threshold. What should be configured?
items.answer: An Azure Monitor alert with an action group
options: An Azure Monitor alert with an action group
items.explanation: Azure Monitor alert rules evaluate telemetry, and action groups specify notifications or automated responses. Locks, storage tiers, and governance hierarchy do not evaluate CPU metrics.

items.prompt: Developers need request rates, exceptions, dependencies, and distributed traces for a web application. Which Azure Monitor feature is designed for this?
items.explanation: Application Insights provides application performance monitoring and traces requests through dependencies. The other services focus on configuration compliance, data governance, or expenditure.
